creator: FreeBSD@ShaneWare.Biz
text:
I setup poudriere a few days ago and have built hydrogen 0.9.6.1 in 8.4 9.3 and 10.0 without issue. Same with tinderbox before.

As for libarchive -
8.4 and 9.3 install /usr/lib/libarchive.so.5
10.0 and 11 install /usr/lib/libarchive.so.6
archivers/libarchive installs /usr/local/lib/libarchive.so.13.1.2

If the port is installed but the library is renamed, leading to the port header and the system library being used then I see the error you show. I can only re-create this error with 0.9.6.1 by manually renaming the port library.

If the port is not installed hydrogen builds using the system libarchive. I don't see any trouble reading the demo files and drumkits available (run tested only on 9.2) so should work fine without the port dependency. I didn't notice the system libarchive before so added the port dependency from my first build.

If the libarchive port is installed before building hydrogen it will be used before the system libarchive. This could lead to a dependency on the port library without pkg knowing about it, allowing the port to be removed breaking the installed hydrogen.
